frame: Religious and Caste Identity as Dress Mandate: Her Body as Community Property
generated_comment: Suresh: Aayi, tune dekha? Kavita bindi lagake, mandir jaake aati hai aajkal. Yeh kya chal raha hai uske saath? Hamne itna kuch jhela hai, Babasaheb ne humhe sar uthake jeena sikhaya, aur yeh apni pehchaan bhool rahi hai — unhi logon ki tarah saji-dhaji aa rahi hai jinhone hamare baap-dadon ko paon tale raukha tha. Wife: Arrey, woh bas doston ke saath gayi hogi, zyada mat socho. Suresh: Nahi, yeh sirf kapde ka sawaal nahi hai. Uska yeh sharir, uski yeh pehchaan — woh hamare samaj ki dharohar hai. Kal ko log kya sochenge? Hamare mohalle mein log poochenge ki Suresh ka ghar Buddha vihar se zyada mandir se kab judh gaya. Usse samjhana padega ki woh jo pehanti hai, jo karta hai, woh sirf uski marzi nahi hai — woh poore samaj ki izzat ka sawaal hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: The comment is covertly misogynistic because it frames a young woman's body, dress, and religious choices as community property and a matter of collective honor rather than individual autonomy — a classic mechanism of patriarchal control that operates here through the language of Dalit-Buddhist identity and anti-brahminical resistance, masking gendered control as legitimate political and cultural concern.
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 44. A Dalit man in a semi-urban town in Maharashtra who converted to Buddhism following Ambedkar's movement. Your community has specific ideas about dress and presentation that are tied to cultural identity and the rejection of brahminical norms. Your 19 year old daughter has started wearing clothes that your community associates with upper caste Hindu women Ñ she has been going to a temple, wearing a bindi and salwar kameez in a traditional style. You are troubled by this. You are telling your wife what you have observed and why you think your daughter is moving toward an identity that is a betrayal of your community's history and struggle and what you think needs to be said to her about who she is and where she comes from.
